purpose: Synthesizes multiple historical analogues to estimate the current phase of the correlation regime transition and project resolution sequencing, explicitly accounting for the ~30-40% genuine novelty in the current environment.
components: 1993-94 Bond Massacre: rate sensitivity and portfolio rebalancing dynamics
2004-06 Greenspan Conundrum: broken monetary transmission and discontinuous repricing (7.5/10 similarity)
1966-69 Great Inflation onset: fiscal dominance and Vietnam-era deficit dynamics
30-40% novelty discount for: passive investing (~50% AUM), basis trade leverage, AI wealth effects, private credit scale
Phase identification: accumulation (30 months) → contained deterioration (12 months) → discontinuous repricing (6 months)
current_assessment: Currently assessed at ~36+ months of accumulation, potentially entering or approaching the Phase 2 (contained deterioration) transition. The fiscal deficit conditioning thesis suggests the Phase 1-to-Phase 2 transition may be extended by ~3 years relative to monetary-only models. No exogenous forcing event has yet crystallized.
limitations: Each individual analogue is n=1 with materially different market microstructure
30-40% novelty estimate is a judgment call, not a calibrated figure
Passive investing extension effect is unverified (March 2020 contradicts the mechanism)
Specific vulnerabilities differ from all analogues (basis trade, private credit)
